assertions.title: Nav renders on initial page load with all expected elements

steps.action: Load http://localhost:3200 at 1440x1400 and capture the initial nav screenshot
steps.expected: A visible nav appears in the viewport with the logo, four section links, GitHub link, and Get Started CTA
steps.observed: The sticky nav rendered at the top of the viewport with visible controls for mktg, Features, Skills, Testimonials, Install, GitHub, and Get Started.

steps.action: Inspect nav children and bounding boxes via DOM evaluation
steps.expected: All expected nav items exist, are visible, and are not clipped
steps.observed: `nav` measured 1440x79px, child controls were visible, and each required item reported `clipped: false` with non-zero bounds.

evidence.network: GET / -> 200 via curl; browser tracked only local Next.js document/font/css/js requests with no failed requests observed.

assertions.title: Nav transitions from transparent to solid on scroll

steps.action: Inspect nav computed styles at scrollY=0
steps.expected: The nav blends into the hero with a transparent or near-transparent background
steps.observed: At the top of the page the nav background was `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)` with `backdrop-filter: none`.

steps.action: Scroll past the hero to scrollY=1700 and inspect nav styles again
steps.expected: The nav gains a solid/semi-transparent dark background with blur
steps.observed: Past the hero, the nav background became `oklab(0.129 -0.0038832 -0.0418201 / 0.8)` with `backdrop-filter: blur(24px)` and a dark shadow.

steps.action: Scroll back to the top and re-check the nav styles
steps.expected: The nav returns to its transparent/near-transparent state
steps.observed: After returning to the top, the nav returned to a near-transparent state with alpha `0.0443409` and minimal blur (`1.33023px`).

evidence.network: No additional network activity was required beyond the initial local document/assets requests.

assertions.title: Anchor links scroll to correct page sections

steps.action: Read the page scroll behavior and click each desktop nav anchor in sequence
steps.expected: Smooth scrolling is enabled and each click lands the target section within 100px of the viewport top
steps.observed: `document.documentElement` reported `scroll-behavior: smooth`; each click scrolled to the matching section with the target top edge at 95.5px.

evidence.network: In-page anchor navigation only; no failed network requests observed.

assertions.title: Logo click scrolls to top of page

steps.action: Scroll to the install section and click the nav wordmark button
steps.expected: The page scrolls back to the very top with scrollY <= 5
steps.observed: Clicking the wordmark button with `aria-label='Scroll to top'` returned the page to `scrollY: 0`.

evidence.network: No network requests beyond existing local asset loads.

assertions.title: Nav CTA button navigates to install section

steps.action: Measure the nav CTA rest styles and hover the button
steps.expected: The nav CTA shows a visible hover-state change
steps.observed: The button background shifted from `oklch(0.765 0.177 163.223)` at rest to `oklch(0.845 0.143 164.978)` on hover.

steps.action: Click the nav Get Started button
steps.expected: The page scrolls to the install section
steps.observed: The click scrolled to `#install` with the section top at 95.5px from the viewport top.

evidence.network: In-page button scroll only; no failed network requests observed.

assertions.title: Nav collapses to hamburger menu on mobile

steps.action: Set viewport to 375x812 and inspect the closed mobile nav
steps.expected: Inline nav links are hidden and a hamburger control is visible
steps.observed: At 375px all five desktop links remained in the DOM but were not visible; the hamburger button was visible at 44x44px with `aria-label='Open navigation menu'`.

steps.action: Open the mobile menu and inspect body scroll lock
steps.expected: The menu shows all section links plus CTA, and page scrolling behind it is locked
steps.observed: Opening the menu exposed Features, Skills, Testimonials, Install, GitHub, and Get Started; `body` switched to `overflow: hidden`, and a wheel-scroll attempt left `scrollY` at 0.

steps.action: Close the menu via toggle, then reopen and close via a nav link
steps.expected: The menu closes both ways and the link close also scrolls to the requested section
steps.observed: Tapping the hamburger again closed the menu and restored `body` overflow to `visible`; tapping Install closed the menu and scrolled the install section into view (`top: -157`, `bottom: 655`, visible in viewport).

evidence.network: Mobile-menu interactions were entirely client-side; no failed network requests observed.

steps.action: Inspect nav layering at the top of the page
steps.expected: The nav renders above hero visuals
steps.observed: At scrollY=0 the nav had `z-index: 60`, and `elementFromPoint` at three sample points across the bar returned nav descendants (`Testimonials`, `mktg`, `Get Started`).

steps.action: Scroll until the terminal demo is behind/under the nav and re-check layering
steps.expected: The nav still renders above the terminal demo and surrounding content
steps.observed: At scrollY=1800, with the demo section spanning `top: -225` to `bottom: 693.5`, the same point checks still resolved to nav descendants and the nav kept `z-index: 60`.

evidence.network: No network activity relevant to layering validation.

assertions.title: Active section highlighted in nav during scroll

steps.action: Inspect nav links at the top of the page
steps.expected: No section link is active while the hero is in view
steps.observed: At `scrollY: 0`, all four section links had `aria-current: null` and no active-state classes.

steps.action: Scroll to each section and inspect active-state markup
steps.expected: Only the current section link is highlighted at a time
steps.observed: At each section top, exactly one matching link carried `aria-current='page'` plus the active classes `bg-slate-900/80 text-emerald-300 ring-1 ring-emerald-400/40`.

evidence.network: No network activity relevant to active-state validation.

frictions.description: `agent-browser scroll down` can bypass CSS scroll locking because it scrolls programmatically; this made it unsuitable for validating the mobile menu's body-scroll lock.
frictions.resolution: Used `agent-browser mouse wheel 500` instead, which kept `scrollY` at 0 while the menu was open and better matched real user input.

summary: Tested 8 navigation assertions on the real localhost:3200 site with browser session 0f916ecfbd72__u1. All 8 assertions passed.
